name: lore_guidance
description: Load the full Lore usage rules. Call this if your context does not already contain detailed usage guidance.

name: lore_status
description: Check memory backend availability and connection health.

name: lore_boot
description: Load the fixed boot memory view that restores the deterministic startup baseline and core operating context.

name: lore_get_node
description: Open a memory node. REQUIRED when opening a URI from a <recall>: copy the exact session_id and query_id from that <recall> tag.

parameters.name: uri
parameters.description: Full memory URI for the node you want to open, such as core://soul. Use core:// or project:// to browse a domain root; bare words are paths in the default domain.

parameters.name: nav_only
parameters.description: If true, skip expensive glossary processing.

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: REQUIRED when the URI came from <recall>: copy the exact session_id from that <recall> tag.

parameters.name: query_id
parameters.description: REQUIRED when the URI came from <recall>: copy the exact query_id from that <recall> tag.

name: lore_search
description: Search memories by keyword, semantic similarity, or both. Returns full content for top results — use this when you need to read memory content directly without a separate get_node call.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query text. Not a wildcard — use a meaningful keyword or phrase. Passing an empty string or * with a domain filter browses that domain root.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Optional domain filter to narrow the search.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results.

parameters.name: content_limit
parameters.description: How many top results include full content (default 5).

name: lore_list_domains
description: Browse the top-level memory domains available in the memory system.

name: lore_create_node
description: Create a new long-term memory concept in the Lore living semantic tree. A URI path names the concept identity with durable snake_case segments; event time belongs in the node narrative or in explicit archive, diary, release, or incident concepts. For multi-segment paths, first make the parent abstraction real with content, disclosure, and glossary, then place the child under that conceptual home. Prefer update or merge when an existing concept already owns the fact.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Memory text body.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: Importance tier (0=core identity, 1=key facts, 2+=general).

parameters.name: glossary
parameters.description: Initial glossary keywords written with this node create event.

parameters.name: uri
parameters.description: Optional final memory URI. It names a durable concept identity; event time belongs in content or in explicit archive, diary, release, or incident concepts. Intermediate paths grow from real parent abstractions with content.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Target memory domain when not using uri.

parameters.name: parent_path
parameters.description: Parent concept path inside the chosen domain; for multi-segment paths this parent abstraction explains why the children belong together and carries content, disclosure, and glossary.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Final concept segment for the new memory; name the reusable idea, module, decision, preference, or archive concept.

parameters.name: disclosure
parameters.description: When this memory should be recalled.

name: lore_update_node
description: Revise an existing long-term memory node. Omitted content, metadata, and glossary mutation fields are left unchanged.

parameters.name: uri
parameters.description: Full memory URI for the node you want to revise.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: New content to replace the existing content; omit to leave content unchanged.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: New priority level; omit to leave priority unchanged.

parameters.name: disclosure
parameters.description: New disclosure / trigger condition; omit to leave disclosure unchanged.

parameters.name: glossary_add
parameters.description: Keywords to add as part of this same node update event.

parameters.name: glossary_remove
parameters.description: Keywords to remove as part of this same node update event.

name: lore_delete_node
description: Remove a memory path that is obsolete, duplicated, or no longer wanted.

parameters.name: uri
parameters.description: Full memory URI for the path you want to remove.

name: lore_move_node
description: Move or rename a memory concept inside the semantic memory tree. The target parent represents the conceptual home; it must already be a real parent abstraction with memory content so the move can reparent the node and its subtree into that abstraction.

parameters.name: old_uri
parameters.description: Current memory URI to move from.

parameters.name: new_uri
parameters.description: New memory URI. For multi-segment paths, the target parent is the parent abstraction that becomes the node conceptual home.
